last_agent_action: Ran the official staged `adf-dev` observe-only Keycloak slice against target `10.167.2.150`, confirmed the split-host path was healthy, and captured a bounded imported-module boundary packet showing Apache login `200`, Keycloak OIDC `503`, `keycloak.service` failed, no `8443` listener, and Metro still alive.

last_validation_results.summary: The live Keycloak-down condition on `10.167.2.150` validated the reusable imported-module boundary packet pilot. The official staged `adf-dev` observe-only slice proved Apache login `200`, Keycloak OIDC `503`, `keycloak.service` failed, no `8443` listener, Metro alive, and a bounded escalation-ready packet. The current wrapper/export step still failed to emit a local runtime-evidence bundle, so the preserved local copy remains audit-only scratch.

Checkpoint-declared delegated result logs and target artifacts now roundtrip through the supplementary runtime-evidence contract. A local proof export for `adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1` carried the checkpoint bundle, delegated result logs, and delegated target artifacts, and the importer-side manifest validator accepted all 75 artifacts.

resume_instructions: Read the objective, backlog, and work-state files before editing code.
Inspect `.pack-state/agent-memory/latest-memory.json` first when it exists, then fall back to other `.pack-state/agent-memory/*.json` files.
Use `docs/specs/adf-autonomous-iteration-loop-completion-boundary-v1.md` as the current contract for what does and does not complete future iteration-loop work.
Use `docs/specs/adf-autonomous-iteration-loop-pilot-evidence-v1.md` as the recorded proof that one real ADF-local iteration happened beyond continuity bookkeeping.

Do not treat continuity bookkeeping, imported runtime-evidence reconcile, restaging, launcher health, or recorded next steps as enough to complete future iteration-loop work without the named pilot evidence artifact.

Do not run `generate-support-baseline` locally on this workspace host to refresh AlgoSec appliance-backed candidates. Use PackFactory-root remote/runtime workflows and preserved remote-backed artifacts for appliance-backed output refresh.

Use `docs/specs/adf-remote-runtime-decoupling-plan-v1.md` as the current split-host contract: PackFactory root orchestrates the remote Codex host, the remote Codex host runs the ADF build-pack runtime, and the remote ADF runtime reaches the separate Rocky target host only when the named task needs target-backed evidence.

Treat strict later-content slice v2 and v3 as control-plane startup stalls, not as content results: both runs wrote only `loop-events.jsonl` with `run_started`, never reached `run-summary.json`, and therefore did not satisfy the blocked-or-checkpoint boundary.

Use `adf-dev` as the current remote Codex host through the existing SSH key authentication path, but keep the local PackFactory build-pack copy as the current precedence source of truth over the remote `adf-dev` build-pack copy until a later explicit remote-canonical mode change is recorded.

The latest accepted delegated AutoDiscovery slice proved two independent faults in the current 502 path: Apache proxies `/AutoDiscovery` through an unresolved `AutoDiscovery` hostname, and `ms-autodiscovery.service` is inactive/dead with no listener on `8095`. Treat that as a deferred follow-up rather than the next blocking step, and restart from `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/autodiscovery-502-observe-v1/target-bundle/` when AutoDiscovery work resumes.
Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-bounded-dependency-map-v1.md` as the current bounded map for the top-level `ASMS UI is down` path.
Use `docs/specs/fireflow-internals-bounded-map-v1.md` as the current bounded note for deeper FireFlow internals behind the first-pass playbook.

Use `docs/specs/asms-docpack-reference-note-v1.md` as the local reference for the verified ASMS docpack on `adf-dev`, but keep the documented caveat in force: the vendor docs are incomplete and should not override live target-backed evidence.

The latest accepted async-branch probe adds a useful negative heuristic: a repeated `CommandsDispatcher` cadence with `3055` and `3070` response sizes can still be journal refresh plus `UserSession` maintenance, not queue-backed FireFlow progression. Keep journal/session interpretation ahead of ActiveMQ for that branch unless a future minute shows a non-`UserSession` destination or same-minute `ms-batch-application` or broker evidence.

The latest accepted stronger FireFlow approval/progression candidate still did not reach ticket mutation. The best in-scope non-`UserSession` branch on `2026-03-26 10:19-10:20 EDT` stayed config-broadcast centered: `ms-configuration` broadcast `application-afaConfig.properties` changes, `ms-initial-plan` received the broadcast, and FireFlow then showed `Authentication:authenticateUser` and `User:GetUserInfo`. Treat that branch as config-propagation plus auth bootstrap, not approval progression, unless a future slice shows a ticket identifier, a dispatcher target beyond `Authentication` or `User`, or same-minute worker or broker evidence.

The latest accepted later FireFlow workflow correlation slice picked one `CommandsDispatcher` minute at `2026-03-26 19:40 EDT` and kept the branch Apache or AFF -> `aff-boot` first, then `ms-configuration` when the same minute rolled into unified swagger refresh and downstream `AutoDiscovery` 502 behavior. Keep ActiveMQ later-supporting for that branch unless future same-minute broker evidence lands on workflow-specific destinations.

The latest accepted home-shell seam slice proved that the hard Metro dependency behind the first usable shell is the backend login/session-bootstrap config fetch through `HandleConfigParams() -> GetConfigurationSettings() -> RestClient::getAllConfig()` against `http://127.0.0.1:8080/afa`, while externally visible `/afa/config` is not the correct appliance control surface.

The latest accepted successful shell-transition slice now gives a reusable baseline window for the next comparison pass: `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php -> home.php -> dynamic.js -> home.js -> dashboard bootstrap commands -> tree bootstrap -> TopbarMenu.php -> prod_stat.php`. Use `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/asms-home-shell-request-correlation-v1/target-bundle/` as the preserved baseline evidence.

The latest accepted failure comparison slice now gives the matching failed branch: `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` on `2026-03-25 12:56:01 EDT` redirects but never reaches `home.php` and falls back to `/algosec-ui/login?last_url=%2FAFA%2Fphp%2FSuiteLoginSessionValidation.php`. Use `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/adf-autonomy-baseline-catch-up-checkpoint-v1/delegated-codex-runs/asms-home-shell-failure-diff-v1/target-bundle/` as the preserved failed baseline.

Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-dependency-order-validation-v1.md` as the canonical conclusion for the top-level ordering claim: do not restate the ASMS path as a strict `host -> httpd -> keycloak -> ms-metro` chain unless future lab evidence disproves the current validation note.

The latest accepted FireFlow-internals slice proves the nearer FireFlow order as Apache route ownership -> `aff-boot` on `1989` -> PostgreSQL, with ActiveMQ and neighboring `algosec-ms.*` services treated as later-supporting unless the symptom is asynchronous, broker-like, or configuration-driven.

Follow the exact local-to-`adf-dev` checkpoint contract in `docs/specs/adf-remote-runtime-decoupling-plan-v1.md`: push accepted local source and instructions to `adf-dev`, pull back bounded run artifacts and candidate changes only at named checkpoints, and accept pulled remote state locally only when the checkpoint bundle explicitly says which work-state, backlog, source, docs, and target-backed artifacts are accepted.

Use one explicit `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json` manifest at checkpoint time so the remote run on `adf-dev` can name the exported runtime-evidence bundle, raw run artifacts, candidate source/docs changes, and exact work-state or backlog fields proposed for local acceptance instead of relying on implicit whole-file comparison.

For the current phase, the remote loop on `adf-dev` should write `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json` into `.pack-state/autonomy-runs/<run-id>/`, copy it into the exported runtime-evidence bundle under `artifacts/` when one exists, and refresh it at the required checkpoint reasons `paused_for_review`, `task_slice_complete`, `evidence_ready`, `blocked_boundary`, and `recovery_snapshot`.

Use the existing build-pack runtime-evidence export path as the default remote-to-root handoff: long-running loops stay on the remote build-pack host, and PackFactory root consumes exported run summaries, loop events, feedback memory, and accepted artifacts instead of acting like a live session proxy to the target host.

Treat the later remote-canonical build-pack mode as the target end state for this line: the remote build pack becomes the canonical runtime home only after the export, checkpoint, and source-of-truth rules are explicit enough to let PackFactory root stay in the orchestrator-and-consumer role.
Start the next cycle from the selected Support Cockpit direction rather than the older reviewed webpage frame.
Preserve the sticky quick-jump behavior because that is the operator-confirmed reason Support Cockpit won the prototype comparison.
Use the cleaned Support Cockpit chassis as the next container for a real playbook iteration instead of reintroducing the older boxed legacy page structures.
The command-first flow strip and the horizontal dependency path now sit above the main two-column layout, and Quick jump starts level with the first stepwise checkpoint content.

Use `docs/specs/adf-diagnostic-mapping-framework-v1.md` as the current small, adjustable operating contract for how ADF should map ASMS runtime surfaces. It is intentionally subject to change if a method proves not useful.

Use `docs/specs/adf-imported-module-drilldown-pilot-v1.md` as the current pilot note for imported-module diagnosis. Treat the pattern as a trial that can be abandoned if it starts producing too much module-specific branching for too little support value.
Use `docs/specs/adf-frontline-playbook-testing-workflow-v1.md` as the current frontline validation contract for `ASMS UI is down`.
Use `docs/specs/adf-frontline-testing-decision-model-v1.md` as the plain-language explanation of why the frontline trial order exists and what counts as a useful stop point.

The current operator-reported Keycloak-down condition on target `10.167.2.150` is a live validation opportunity for the imported-module drilldown pilot, but it is not accepted ADF evidence until it is captured through the normal remote/runtime evidence path.

When a bounded imported-module drilldown needs deeper interpretation, upstream open source docs and repositories may be cited as supplementary guidance after local runtime evidence and ASMS integration evidence establish the boundary.
Use `docs/specs/adf-httpd-shallow-fault-validation-plan-v1.md` as the recorded fail-closed contract for the first mutation-backed frontline trial.

Use the current frontline validation sequence for `ASMS UI is down`: healthy-path trial first, symptom-classification trial second, and only then a safe reversible shallow-fault trial if the earlier phases are already useful.
Use `docs/specs/adf-remote-autonomy-testing-workflow-v1.md` as the current contract for testing whether Codex on `adf-dev` can continue a real ADF task with bounded supervision.

The first real `adf-dev` continuity evaluation is now complete and preserved in `eval/history/adf-dev-remote-autonomy-continuity-evaluation-20260328.md`. It exposed the original prompt-scope and benchmark-output contract mismatches.

The post-fix rerun is now preserved in `eval/history/adf-dev-remote-autonomy-contract-fix-rerun-20260328.md`. The two targeted blockers are fixed: the prompt now targets the current canonical task and the loop exports a bundle without the earlier benchmark-output boundary violation.

The bounded real-task continuity slice on 2026-03-28 is now preserved in `eval/history/adf-dev-deepen-dependency-continuity-slice-20260328.md`: the loop scoped correctly, exported a bundle, and stopped at `current_task_incomplete` without a content checkpoint.

The v3 `adf-dev` contract-safe shell-versus-Metro slice is now proven and imported canonically: preserve `2026-03-26 05:29:53-05:29:56 EDT` as the exact shell-transition evidence window showing `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php -> /afa/php/home.php` plus same-window Metro `config` and `session/extend` clues.

Treat the current raw bounded remote loop as contract-safe only for the declared state, history, memory, candidate-output, and runtime-evidence surfaces. Do not rerun it expecting `src/` or `docs/` authoring until a reviewed contract change explicitly allows that wider write scope.
Treat `deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks` as the active parent track again; the contract-safe remote slice no longer blocks that content path.

The March 28 exporter/importer update now closes the earlier checkpoint-artifact completeness gap: supplementary runtime-evidence bundles can carry checkpoint-declared delegated result logs and target artifacts, and the importer-side manifest validator accepts that widened contract.

The March 28 recovered later-content slice v4 now satisfies the fresh later-content checkpoint: at `2026-03-28 17:56 EDT`, `/afa/php/home.php?segment=DEVICES` was already present and `POST /afa/php/commands.php` returned the `GET_ANALYSIS_OPTIONS` dialog body for device `200132`.

The first `adf-dev` later-content branch slice on March 28 stopped too permissively: it reused preserved timestamps and local Step 4 wording, produced no recoverable runtime-evidence bundle, and should be treated as fail-closed debugging evidence only.

The March 28 launcher-hardening proof run v2 is now the accepted control-plane proof: the remote slice wrote `run-summary.json`, `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json`, and an exported runtime-evidence bundle, and the local wrapper pulled the bundle back cleanly.
Treat `capture_fresh_later_content_branch_checkpoint` as complete. The March 28 v4 recovered import preserved the later-content boundary itself.

The stricter v2 and v3 retries both reproduced the same startup-stall pattern even after the invocation prompt started carrying the request `remote_reason`, so prompt specificity alone did not resolve the issue.

The first diagnosis rerun showed that even the startup-stall diagnosis task can be too broad for the remote loop. Prefer the child task `compare_successful_vs_stalled_remote_run_shapes` so the next `adf-dev` slice has one small comparison checkpoint and a clear stop rule.

The compare-first diagnosis slice did produce its target artifact on March 28: `run-shape-comparison.json` names the stalled boundary as `pre_invocation_control_plane_startup_stall` and points the next fix toward `stabilize_remote_codex_launcher_against_interactive_menu`.

Treat the compare-first diagnosis result as locally reviewed autonomy evidence rather than imported external runtime evidence, because the outer SSH controller still timed out before the remote side wrote `run-summary.json`, `adf-remote-checkpoint-bundle.json`, or an export bundle.

The first raw bounded run on that narrowed task is now preserved under `.pack-state/remote-autonomy-roundtrips/.../algosec-diagnostic-framework-build-pack-v1-contract-safe-shell-metro-slice-v1/`. It proved the slice can finish without writable-surface drift, but the returned bundle still lacked the intended explicit checkpoint-bundle artifact.

The March 28 late-export recovery proof run v6 now closes that follow-up: with a `5s` first delayed pullback and bounded late-export recovery attempts enabled, the wrapper still returned a normal pulled bundle instead of failing during pullback.
The v2 and v3 reruns together now prove this path can write, export, pull, import, and reconcile the explicit checkpoint bundle while returning a support-useful shell-versus-Metro evidence minute.

Track the leftover `starter_backlog_incomplete` terminal wording as the later planner task `align_remote_loop_terminal_state_semantics_with_current_task_contract`; it is a scoring and clarity refinement, not the blocker we just fixed.

Score a frontline trial by whether it stays on host sanity, Apache or HTTPD, core service status, safe restart boundaries, and first usable shell classification without drifting into deeper auth/bootstrap or downstream module theory.

The healthy-path trial and the symptom-classification trial are now both complete and passed: use `eval/history/asms-ui-frontline-healthy-path-trial-20260327.md` plus `eval/history/asms-ui-frontline-symptom-classification-trial-20260327.md` before planning the next phase.

The first shallow-fault planning pass is now complete and it chose `httpd` as the first mutation-backed service boundary because it is the clearest top-level UI edge check with straightforward rollback.
The first `httpd` shallow-fault execution pass is now complete and passed: use `eval/history/asms-ui-httpd-shallow-fault-validation-20260328.md` before planning the next follow-up.
The `httpd` shallow-fault validation is now recorded and it passed. The observer stopped cleanly at the Apache or HTTPD edge boundary, and rollback restored the baseline successfully.

The next recommended content task is again `deepen_dependency_aware_playbooks`, and the corrected `adf-dev` loop can now be used to evaluate that real task without the two earlier remote-loop contract mismatches.

The canonical cross-playbook ADF taxonomy is ASMS entry and edge -> ASMS authentication and session -> ASMS application services -> shared runtime and dependencies -> host integration and operating evidence.
Treat `useful work` as a stop rule that tells the engineer where to stop, not as a named layer in the taxonomy.
Keep mapping cycles small. A mapping slice is only worth keeping when it proves or eliminates a seam, narrows the next stop point, or upgrades a vague symptom into a named support boundary.
Keep treating the current ASMS UI page as a first real system-thinking reference playbook and tighten it through review rather than reverting to the older incremental patch model.

The current ASMS UI service pack still carries real Keycloak and ms-metro deeper checks, but those now belong behind the shallow service and first-shell path rather than in the default frontline sequence.

For the current pilot, the next useful Keycloak change is a reusable module-boundary drilldown that helps classify startup, dependency, resource, useful-work-path, and escalation-ready unknown cases instead of another one-off Keycloak scenario page.
Treat ActiveMQ as a supporting dependency for the ASMS UI path, not a first-pass UI-down check, unless ms-metro logs or behavior point to JMS or broker problems.

Treat the current four playbooks as seed traversals of the shared taxonomy, not as the limit of ADF. Future playbooks should inherit the same ASMS-runtime-first layers and only vary the symptom entry point, likely traversal, safe restarts, and stop conditions.

The current evidence-backed ASMS UI working model now starts shallow: ASMS entry and edge -> core service status and safe restart boundary -> first usable shell classification -> deeper auth, BusinessFlow, FireFlow, or AFA seams only when the shallow path stays ambiguous or the reproduced journey clearly reaches them.
The systems-thinking method is now proven on the lab: reproduce one bounded browser flow, correlate the same minute in Apache and downstream logs, and only then update the playbook model.
Keep observed and inferred dependency claims separate. The value comes from reducing support effort with real path evidence, not from forcing a cleaner serial chain than the lab actually shows.

Treat disk, memory, OOM, and other core host-resource checks as the shared Host Health Pre-Check pack defined in docs/specs/adf-host-health-precheck-pack-v1.md and use it at the start of every major playbook.
Keep the stronger Keycloak and ms-metro command coverage, but use those checks only after the shallow service and first-shell path has already narrowed the case to auth or app behavior.

Do not promote BusinessFlow or FireFlow in the default frontline `ASMS UI is down` path. Keep them as conditional downstream seams only after the customer-visible path or reproduced journey clearly reaches them.
Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-backward-dependency-trajectory-note-20260326.md` as historical engineering context only, not as the default frontline trajectory for `ASMS UI is down`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-fireflow-dependency-seams-pass-20260326.md` as an escalation-only seam note: BusinessFlow's closest readable neighbors are Postgres, AFA, and AFF, while FireFlow's closest readable neighbors are Apache proxying, aff-boot, and database-backed runtime behavior.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-health-implementation-pass-20260326.md` as the current BusinessFlow deep-health note: the `AFA connection` and `AFF connection` checks are local HTTPS session-liveness checks rather than raw direct 8080 or 1989 probes.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-local-https-ownership-pass-20260326.md` as the local-HTTPS ownership note: the BusinessFlow AFF side is now reduced to Apache `443` -> `aff-boot` `1989`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-afa-local-ownership-pass-20260326.md` as the AFA local-ownership note: `/afa/php/...` is Apache-fronted local PHP, while `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` are Metro-backed proxy families.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-businessflow-afa-soap-path-pass-20260326.md` as the final AFA path note: the BusinessFlow `AFA connection` deep-health check lands on `https://localhost/afa/php/ws.php` and uses SOAP `isSessionAlive`, not `/afa/php/commands.php`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-afa-soap-runtime-correlation-pass-20260326.md` as the runtime seam note: Apache only gives timing for `ws.php`, while `.ht-fa-history` provides the request hash, `SESSION / TOKEN / COOKIE` triple, and `is_session_alive` result, and the next direct seam is the PHP session file plus `.ht-LastActionTime` for that same session id.

Treat `httpd.service`, `keycloak.service`, `ms-metro.service`, and their shallow listener or restart boundaries as the first support-relevant service surfaces for `ASMS UI is down`; only move to deeper backend seams when those shorter checks do not explain the case.

The first healthy-path frontline trial is now recorded and it stayed shallow. The observed window stopped at host sanity, Apache or HTTPD edge, core service status, shallow listeners, and localhost UI reachability without needing deeper auth or subsystem checks.

The symptom-classification trial is now also recorded and it stayed shallow. The observed result reclassified the case as external path or browser-side trouble when localhost still served the UI edge and login shell cleanly.
The next best test is to execute the planned lab-only `httpd` shallow-fault validation with a separate observer path that stops at the Apache or HTTPD edge boundary.

Keep ActiveMQ behind those module checkpoints for now. It is now a proven later supporting dependency for the FireFlow seam because aff-boot holds live broker connections on 61616, but the current lab evidence still keeps it behind the closer route, service, and database checks instead of treating it as a first-pass `GUI down` gate.

Within the BusinessFlow seam, keep the new deep-health implementation result in view: `AFA connection` and `AFF connection` are local HTTPS session-liveness checks, so the immediate seam stays closer to Apache and those app routes than to raw 8080, 1989, or broker theory.

Treat the BusinessFlow seam as reduced enough to name directly in the current playbook: `AFA connection` lands on Apache-served local PHP SOAP `/afa/php/ws.php`, and `AFF connection` lands on Apache `443` -> `aff-boot` `1989` via `/FireFlow/api/session`.

Carry forward `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-main-stop-logic-refinement-pass-20260326.md` as historical stop-logic context only. The current frontline stop logic is now shallower: Step 3 is core services and safe restart boundaries, Step 4 is first usable shell classification, and Step 5 is escalation-only deeper correlation.

For the AFA side, stop the next support step at `.ht-fa-history` and the PHP session backing files before jumping outward to Metro. The first useful follow-on seam is `ws.php` -> `.ht-fa-history` -> `/var/lib/php/session/sess_<id>` -> `.ht-LastActionTime`.

Within the FireFlow seam, keep Apache proxying, aff-boot, and database-backed runtime evidence ahead of ActiveMQ for first-pass troubleshooting. Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-fireflow-broker-dependency-pass-20260326.md` plus `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-fireflow-broker-correlation-pass-20260326.md` as the supporting notes: aff-boot holds live broker connections on 61616, but the reproduced ASMS login-handoff minute still centered on auth, session, and REST activity instead of broker-side signals.

Keep the first post-home checks inside the ASMS playbook for now, but demote them to supporting clues: `FireFlowBridge.js`, basic home assets, and nearby Metro routes help explain the same minute after `/afa/php/home.php`, but they are not first-class gates unless the rendered home shell itself degrades.

Within those nearby Metro routes, treat `GET /afa/getStatus` as the strongest immediate supporting clue after the first usable shell appears. Keep `GET /afa/api/v1/license`, `GET /afa/api/v1/config?...`, `GET /afa/api/v1/config/all/noauth?domain=0`, and `POST /afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` as same-minute but still unproven first-shell requirements until a fresh-session isolation pass proves one of them matters.

The latest fresh-session pass now ties `GET /afa/api/v1/config?...` and `POST /afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` more directly to the new `PHPSESSID`, so they should be treated as stronger fresh-session clues than before, but still not as proven first-shell gates.

A later CDP `Network.setBlockedURLs(...)` pass matched only the early `config/PRINT_TABLE_IN_NORMAL_VIEW` probe and did not record real block events for the fresh-session `license`, `config`, `config/all/noauth`, or `session/extend` routes, so browser-side blocking is not yet a reliable control point for those requests on this lab.

The route-ownership pass now shows Apache exposing separate `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` proxy families toward Metro, while the fresh-session minute showed paired `/afa/config/...` and `/afa/api/v1/config?...` surfaces on the server side.

Variant A of the Apache seam mutation has now run. Denying `/afa/external` returned repeated `403` responses for that family but still left `/afa/php/home.php` fully usable, so `/afa/external` is now demoted as a first-shell gate candidate.

Variant B of the Apache seam mutation has also run. Denying the top-level `/afa/api/v1` Location still left `/afa/php/home.php` fully usable, and same-minute Metro logs still showed fresh-session `/afa/api/v1/config?...` and `/afa/api/v1/session/extend?...` as `200`, so the top-level Apache deny is not a clean control lever for that path.

Keep `POST /afa/api/v1/bridge/refresh?...` demoted from the ASMS first-shell gate model for now because the latest lab pass tied it to a different long-lived session instead of the reproduced fresh login session.

The newer route-owner split is now explicit: Apache owns browser-facing `/afa/external` and `/afa/api/v1` proxy families, while PHP `RestClient` also issues direct `http://127.0.0.1:8080/afa/config/...` requests.
Treat the remaining `config` question as browser-owned versus PHP-owned route ownership, not as another family-wide Apache deny candidate.
Prefer one read-only correlation pass that proves which Metro `config` requests have no matching Apache parent before planning another mutation.

That read-only correlation pass is now complete for fresh session `jkp0cnnf971ksgifu3k25jopiq`: browser-facing `/afa/external/...` lines matched Metro `/afa/api/v1/...`, while direct `/afa/config/...` and same-minute `/afa/getStatus` lines had no Apache parent.
Treat the remaining unresolved seam as mostly the internal direct `/afa/config/...` family behind PHP `RestClient`, not as another browser-facing proxy-family question.

The smallest auth-side named read is now already demoted by observation: `GET /afa/config/Case_Sensitive_Username?...` returns `404` on the lab and `SuiteLoginSessionValidation.php` already falls back without breaking login.
Treat the broader `AlgosecSession::__construct()` / `GetConfigurationSettings()` bundle as the first unresolved internal config surface worth planning next.

The broader bundle should now be approached through login bootstrap, not `home.php`: `home.php` mostly consumes an existing `AlgosecSession`, while `AlgosecSessionManager::executeLogin()` and `postLoginActions()` own the first bootstrap bundle loads.

Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-login-bootstrap-control-path-pass-20260326.md` before planning the next internal mutation so the first app-level experiment starts at the bootstrap hook instead of another coarse transport seam.
Use `docs/specs/asms-ui-login-bootstrap-config-isolation-plan-v1.md` as the closed record of the bootstrap-bundle experiment family.

Treat Variant C1, Variant C2, and Variant C3 as all reduced for first-shell gating: skipping the post-login recache, bypassing the constructor-time bundle, and suppressing both together still left `/afa/php/home.php` usable on the lab.

Use `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-postlogin-handleconfigparams-variant-c1-pass-20260326.md`, `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-constructor-config-bypass-variant-c2-pass-20260326.md`, and `.pack-state/remote-codex/asms-ui-combined-bootstrap-variant-c3-pass-20260326.md` as the canonical bootstrap results.
